text.title: Tool Description Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in tool catalogs that the audit rubric catches. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Description starts with adjectives ("Our powerful, AI-driven, enterprise-grade ..."). Tokens-per-description > 200 with low discrimination signal.

root-cause.source: Internal — copying from public marketing pages

antipattern.root-cause: Author reused the API marketing blurb because it was already written. Model has no use for any of those words.
antipattern.fix: Replace with the 5-part structure: "Hybrid search over the docs corpus. Keyword plus semantic. Returns top 10 passages with paths." Strip all adjectives.

antipattern.symptom: Two tools have similar verbs (read_file / view_file / open_file). Eval shows 30-50% mis-selection rate between them on identical inputs.
antipattern.root-cause: Neither description names the other; model alternates near-randomly when both look plausible.
antipattern.fix: Add mutual "do NOT use this when — use `<other_tool>` instead" lines that point to the partner by exact name.

antipattern.symptom: Destructive tool (`delete_branch`, `drop_table`) described without `MUTATING:` marker. Agent calls it on the current working branch.
antipattern.root-cause: Verb alone is not a strong-enough signal; safety hooks can't pattern-match on description.
antipattern.fix: Prefix description with `MUTATING:` (or `IRREVERSIBLE:`); pair with the dry-run / preview tool's name; CI fails a description with destructive verb but no marker.

antipattern.symptom: Description mentions `page` argument; JSON schema has no `page` field (or vice versa). Agent fills phantom arguments → API rejects request.

root-cause.source: Anthropic tool-use docs; internal argument-fill audits

antipattern.root-cause: Description and schema updated by different commits; CI never cross-checks.
antipattern.fix: Lint rule: parse description for argument names; require each to appear in `input_schema.properties`. Fail build on drift.

antipattern.symptom: Paginated tool description lacks per-page count + max-page cap. Agent calls 30+ pages enumerating a corpus instead of narrowing the query.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought enumeration was the agent's choice; without an explicit cap the model defaults to brute-force.
antipattern.fix: Add "Returns N per page; pass cursor from previous response. Do NOT loop more than M pages — narrow the query if you need more." to the description.
